hints.junior: If a bigger z-index doesn't fix it, you're trapped in a stacking context. The fix isn't a number — it's moving the element outside the context.
hints.mid: The first thing to check: does any ancestor have transform, opacity < 1, or will-change? Those silently create stacking contexts.
hints.staff: Audit your component library for transform: translateZ(0) on animation wrappers — that pattern creates stacking contexts that trap modals and tooltips.

hints.junior: See how the modal hides behind the colored box in broken mode? That box has a transform on it — creating a stacking context. Portal mode renders the modal outside that context.
hints.mid: Check the DOM in Elements panel. Fixed mode: modal is a direct body child. Broken mode: modal is nested inside the transform container.
hints.staff: DevTools Layers panel (3D view) is the fastest way to visualize the stacking context tree — each composited layer is its own context.

hints.junior: isolation: isolate is the clean way to create a stacking context on purpose — it has zero visual effect unlike opacity or transform.
hints.mid: A z-index token scale prevents the 9999 arms race. Define --z-modal, --z-tooltip, --z-dropdown once and reference them everywhere.
hints.staff: Portal rendering is the architectural solution — it decouples component logical position from DOM physical position, which is the right mental model for overlay components.

anecdotes.junior: I once spent three hours debugging a dropdown that was hidden by a card. The card had opacity: 0.99 — added by a designer to 'soften' a background. One stacking context, one missed dropdown.
anecdotes.mid: The will-change: transform pattern for GPU compositing is useful for animations but creates stacking contexts. Teams add it for perf and forget the side effect.
anecdotes.staff: Headless UI and Radix UI both use portals for overlays by default. When teams fork those components and remove the portal, this bug reliably re-emerges.

sections.junior: Hey! Today we're solving the 'z-index 9999 still doesn't work' mystery. It's about stacking contexts — they're not obvious, but once you understand them, modal bugs disappear.
sections.mid: This case is about CSS stacking contexts — why z-index has no effect against them, what creates them accidentally, and the portal pattern that escapes them.
sections.staff: We're covering the full stacking context trigger list, isolation: isolate as the semantic solution, and the design system z-index token scale pattern.
